https://github.com/andrewjiang/palantir-for-family-trips This is similiar to what I want to create. I want to create a palantir like dashboard. But it will track people and equipment as the will be used by a comm squadron to track their people and assets. locations are worldwide. I need you to help me create a MAVEN like dashboard that can have all this date inputted to track everything from a leadership perspective
</user_query>

content.text:
Reference project: dark “command center” UI (React 19, Vite, Google Maps, Framer Motion), timeline + map + local browser state. Your use case needs multi-entity ops tracking and likely multi-user leadership views. Clarifying two decisions that strongly affect architecture:

[REDACTED]

questions.prompt: Where must this run? (This determines maps, hosting, and whether we use cloud APIs.)

options.label: Government or restricted network (limited or no public cloud / API keys)
Not sure yet — propose a default path

questions.prompt: When you say “MAVEN-like,” what do you mean most?

options.label: Palantir-style ops room: dark UI, map + timeline + dense panels (like the family-trip repo vibe)
Match a specific Palantir/Maven product or internal tool (you’ll share screenshots or requirements later)
Leadership KPI board: readiness, accountability, locations at a glance (less “movie UI”)

content.text:
Drafting a plan for a commercial, internet-connected Palantir-style ops dashboard for people and equipment tracking.

[REDACTED]

input.name: Comm squadron ops dashboard
input.overview: Greenfield web app: Palantir-style dark command UI (map + timeline + dense leadership panels) to track people, equipment, and worldwide locations for a communications squadron, deployed on commercial internet with room to grow into multi-user persistence.
input.plan:
# Comm squadron Palantir-style leadership dashboard

## North star

Build a **desktop-first, dark “ops room” dashboard** (same energy as [andrewjiang/palantir-for-family-trips](https://github.com/andrewjiang/palantir-for-family-trips): map-centric, timeline playback, dramatic density) but with a **domain model for command leadership**: who is where, what gear is where, what is degraded or overdue, and what changed recently—**globally**.

## Reference patterns to borrow (not copy)

From the family-trip repo README: **React + Vite**, **Google Maps JS API**, **Framer Motion**, **Lucide** icons, **local state first**—then extend with real persistence and roles because your problem is organizational, not a single weekend itinerary.

## Recommended stack (fits your “commercial online” choice)

| Layer | Choice | Why |
|-------|--------|-----|
| UI | React + Vite + **TypeScript** | Same as reference, safer for evolving ops data |
| Map | **Google Maps JavaScript API** | Familiar, strong global basemap; API key in env |
| Motion | Framer Motion | Panel transitions, timeline scrub affordances |
| Icons | Lucide | Matches reference, fast |
| Styling | **CSS variables + layout grid** (optional: Tailwind if you want speed) | Dark theme tokens, panel chrome |
| Auth (v1.5) | **Auth provider** (e.g. Clerk, Auth0) or simple org SSO later | Leadership data needs access control |
| API + DB (v2) | **PostgreSQL** + **PostGIS** (locations), REST or tRPC | Worldwide coordinates, filters, audit trail |

**MVP default:** start **frontend-only** with a typed in-memory store + `localStorage` export/import (like the reference’s browser state), so you can iterate on UX fast; add **API + DB** when multiple leaders need one truth.

## Core information model (entities)

Design around objects leadership actually queries:

- **Person**: name, rank/role, unit (squadron/flight/team), contact fields (policy-dependent), **current location** (lat/lng + label), **status** (e.g. TDY, leave, training, deployed, garrison), **orders/remarks**, **last verified time**.
- **Equipment**: NSN/serial (optional), nomenclature, **custodian** (person or org), **home station**, **current location**, **condition/serviceability**, **calibration/due dates**, **accountability doc** refs (optional text).
- **Location / site** (optional normalized): base name, ICAO, country, timezone—reuse for consistent map pins and reporting.
- **Event / movement** (timeline backbone): “Person A arrived B,” “Asset X shipped,” “Status changed”—each with **timestamp**, **actor** (user or system), **before/after** snapshot ids for audit.
- **Alert / exception** (derived): overdue maintenance, stale location (>N days), personnel overdue check-in, equipment not linked to a custodian.

Keep a single **“operational picture”** selector: time **now** vs **replay** at time T (timeline scrub updates map + panels).

## Leadership-facing surfaces (screens)

1. **Command map (primary)**  
   Layers toggles: People / Equipment / Sites / Routes (optional). Cluster at low zoom; detail cards on selection. **Worldwide** fit bounds on filtered set.

2. **Timeline / playback**  
   Horizontal scrubber of events; “play” mode animates map state (same idea as the reference’s route playback, but driven by movement/status events).

3. **Roster boards**  
   Dense tables: filter by flight, status, country, “stale location,” equipment serviceability. Export CSV for staff drills.

4. **Equipment readiness panel**  
   Roll-ups: % mission capable, overdue PMEL/cal, high-value items not geolocated.

5. **Personnel accountability panel**  
   Counts by status/region; exceptions queue; click-through to entity.

6. **Data entry / admin**  
   Forms for person + equipment CRUD, bulk CSV import, and **manual location update** with map pick (leaders will tolerate friction if it’s trustworthy).

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph ui [Leadership UI]
    Map[CommandMap]
    Time[TimelinePlayback]
    Roster[RosterAndBoards]
    Admin[DataEntryAndImport]
  end
  subgraph data [Data layer]
    Store[EntityStore]
    Events[EventLog]
    Alerts[DerivedAlerts]
  end
  Map --> Store
  Time --> Events
  Roster --> Store
  Admin --> Store
  Store --> Events
  Store --> Alerts
```

## Phased delivery

**Phase A — UX prototype (1–2 weeks of focused build)**  
Project scaffold, theme shell, mock data seed, map + selection + side panels, timeline with synthetic events, `localStorage` import/export JSON.

**Phase B — “Real org” MVP**  
Postgres schema matching entities above, API, **login + roles** (viewer vs editor vs admin), audit log for edits, CSV import jobs.

**Phase C — Operational hardening**  
Soft-delete, data retention policy hooks, optional integrations with spreadsheets/tools you already use (one direction at a time), saved views (filters) per leader.

## Security and data hygiene (even on “commercial online”)

- Treat all fields as sensitive by default: **encrypt at rest** when DB lands, **TLS**, **role-based access**, and avoid embedding secrets in the client.  
- Decide early what is **OK to store** vs **forbidden** (PII, operational details); the UI can still demo with synthetic data.

## Repository layout (suggested)

New repo (or your workspace root):

- `apps/web/` — Vite React app  
- `packages/core/` (optional) — shared types, validators (Zod), alert rules  
- Later: `apps/api/` + `db/migrations/`

Key files to mirror the reference’s intent (names illustrative):

- `apps/web/src/App.tsx` — shell, layout grid, overlay composition  
- `apps/web/src/components/CommandMap.tsx` — map layers, interactions  
- `apps/web/src/domain/model.ts` — types + seed data  
- `apps/web/src/domain/events.ts` — timeline + replay helpers  

## What you will need to provide during build

- **Example workflows** leadership runs weekly (accountability call, equipment roll-up, deployment tracking).  
- **Status enums** you actually use (keep the first version small).  
- **Whether locations** are usually **base + country** vs **exact coordinates** (both can be supported).

## Success criteria

- A leader can answer in under a minute: **where are my people and high-value kits, what’s wrong, what changed since last shift**.  
- Map + timeline stay in sync; filters are fast; data entry does not require engineers.

todos.content: Scaffold Vite + React + TS app; env for Google Maps key; dark theme tokens
Define Zod schemas + TS types for Person, Equipment, Site, Event; seed + JSON import/export
Implement CommandMap with layers, selection cards, clustering at low zoom
Implement event log + playback/scrub tied to map + panel state
Roster + equipment readiness tables with filters and CSV export
CRUD forms and map pick for location updates
Add Postgres+PostGIS API, auth, and audit log when multi-user truth is required

input.contents:
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "Bundler",
    "declaration": true,
    "declarationMap": true,
    "outDir": "dist",
    "rootDir": "src",
    "strict": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true
  },
  "include": ["src/**/*.ts"]
}

import type { OpsDataset } from "./schemas.js";

const iso = (d: Date) => d.toISOString();

export function createSeedDataset(): OpsDataset {
  const now = new Date();
  const sites = [
    {
      id: "site-andersen",
      name: "Andersen AFB",
      icao: "PGUA",
      country: "Guam",
      timezone: "Pacific/Guam",
      coordinates: { lat: 13.584, lng: 144.93 },
    },
    {
      id: "site-ramstein",
      name: "Ramstein AB",
      icao: "ETAR",
      country: "Germany",
      timezone: "Europe/Berlin",
      coordinates: { lat: 49.436, lng: 7.6 },
    },
    {
      id: "site-tinker",
      name: "Tinker AFB",
      icao: "KTIK",
      country: "United States",
      timezone: "America/Chicago",
      coordinates: { lat: 35.415, lng: -97.386 },
    },
    {
      id: "site-elmendorf",
      name: "Joint Base Elmendorf-Richardson",
      icao: "PAED",
      country: "United States",
      timezone: "America/Anchorage",
      coordinates: { lat: 61.25, lng: -149.8 },
    },
  ];

  const people = [
    {
      id: "p-101",
      name: "Maj Alex Rivera",
      rankRole: "Flight Commander",
      unit: "Alpha Flight",
      status: "deployed" as const,
      locationLabel: "Guam",
      coordinates: sites[0].coordinates,
      siteId: "site-andersen",
      ordersRemarks: "COMM package lead",
      lastVerifiedAt: iso(new Date(now.getTime() - 2 * 60 * 60 * 1000)),
    },
    {
      id: "p-102",
      name: "TSgt Jamie Chen",
      rankRole: "NCOIC",
      unit: "Alpha Flight",
      status: "tdy" as const,
      locationLabel: "Ramstein AB",
      coordinates: sites[1].coordinates,
      siteId: "site-ramstein",
      ordersRemarks: "SATCOM maintenance node",
      lastVerifiedAt: iso(new Date(now.getTime() - 26 * 60 * 60 * 1000)),
    },
    {
      id: "p-103",
      name: "SrA Morgan Ellis",
      rankRole: "Technician",
      unit: "Bravo Flight",
      status: "garrison" as const,
      locationLabel: "Tinker AFB",
      coordinates: sites[2].coordinates,
      siteId: "site-tinker",
      lastVerifiedAt: iso(new Date(now.getTime() - 30 * 60 * 1000)),
    },
    {
      id: "p-104",
      name: "Capt Sam Patel",
      rankRole: "Ops Officer",
      unit: "HQ",
      status: "training" as const,
      locationLabel: "JBER",
      coordinates: sites[3].coordinates,
      siteId: "site-elmendorf",
      ordersRemarks: "Leadership course",
      lastVerifiedAt: iso(new Date(now.getTime() - 4 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000)),
    },
  ];

  const equipment = [
    {
      id: "e-201",
      nomenclature: "SATCOM Terminal (Demo)",
      serialOrId: "SN-DEMO-88421",
      custodian: "Maj Alex Rivera",
      homeStation: "Tinker AFB",
      locationLabel: "Guam",
      coordinates: sites[0].coordinates,
      siteId: "site-andersen",
      condition: "fully_mission_capable" as const,
      calibrationDueAt: iso(new Date(now.getTime() + 45 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000)),
    },
    {
      id: "e-202",
      nomenclature: "Tactical Radio Set",
      serialOrId: "TR-992",
      custodian: "TSgt Jamie Chen",
      homeStation: "Ramstein AB",
      locationLabel: "Ramstein AB",
      coordinates: sites[1].coordinates,
      siteId: "site-ramstein",
      condition: "partially_mission_capable" as const,
      calibrationDueAt: iso(new Date(now.getTime() - 3 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000)),
    },
    {
      id: "e-203",
      nomenclature: "Server Stack (Mobile)",
      custodian: "Bravo Flight",
      homeStation: "Tinker AFB",
      locationLabel: "Tinker AFB",
      coordinates: { lat: 35.42, lng: -97.38 },
      siteId: "site-tinker",
      condition: "maintenance" as const,
    },
  ];

  const events = [
    {
      id: "ev-1",
      at: iso(new Date(now.getTime() - 6 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000)),
      kind: "movement" as const,
      summary: "Alpha Flight lead repositioned to Andersen",
      entityType: "person" as const,
      entityId: "p-101",
      actor: "Ops Desk",
    },
    {
      id: "ev-2",
      at: iso(new Date(now.getTime() - 4 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000)),
      kind: "status_change" as const,
      summary: "SrA Ellis returned to garrison status",
      entityType: "person" as const,
      entityId: "p-103",
    },
    {
      id: "ev-3",
      at: iso(new Date(now.getTime() - 2 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000)),
      kind: "equipment_condition" as const,
      summary: "Tactical Radio Set degraded — awaiting parts",
      entityType: "equipment" as const,
      entityId: "e-202",
      actor: "Maintenance",
    },
    {
      id: "ev-4",
      at: iso(new Date(now.getTime() - 8 * 60 * 60 * 1000)),
      kind: "movement" as const,
      summary: "SATCOM terminal confirmed on station Guam",
      entityType: "equipment" as const,
      entityId: "e-201",
    },
  ];

  return {
    version: 1,
    sites,
    people,
    equipment,
    events,
    updatedAt: iso(now),
  };
}

  id: z.string(),
  name: z.string(),
  rankRole: z.string(),
  unit: z.string(),
  contactNote: z.string().optional(),
  status: PersonStatusSchema,
  locationLabel: z.string(),
  coordinates: GeoPointSchema,

  homeCoordinates: GeoPointSchema.optional(),

  ordersRemarks: z.string().optional(),
  lastVerifiedAt: z.string(),
});

content.text:
Creating the web app: `apps/web` with Vite, components, and API.

[REDACTED]

input.contents:
{
  "name": "apps/web",
  "private": true,
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "tsc -b && vite build",
    "preview": "vite preview"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@comm-ops/core": "*",
    "@googlemaps/js-api-loader": "^1.16.8",
    "@googlemaps/markerclusterer": "^2.5.3",
    "framer-motion": "^11.11.17",
    "lucide-react": "^0.460.0",
    "react": "^19.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.0.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/google.maps": "^3.58.1",
    "@types/react": "^19.0.1",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.0.1",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.3.3",
    "typescript": "~5.6.2",
    "vite": "^5.4.11"
  }
}

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "useDefineForClassFields": true,
    "lib": ["ES2022", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"],
    "module": "ESNext",
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "moduleResolution": "Bundler",
    "allowImportingTsExtensions": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "moduleDetection": "force",
    "noEmit": true,
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "strict": true,
    "noUnusedLocals": true,
    "noUnusedParameters": true,
    "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true,
    "noUncheckedSideEffectImports": true,
    "baseUrl": ".",
    "paths": {
      "@comm-ops/core": ["../../packages/core/src/index.ts"]
    }

content.text:
Here’s what was implemented and how to run it.

### What you have

**Monorepo** under [`C:\Users\Sunwoo\.cursor\projects\empty-window`](file:///C:/Users/Sunwoo/.cursor/projects/empty-window):

| Area | Purpose |
|------|--------|
| [`packages/core`](file:///C:/Users/Sunwoo/.cursor/projects/empty-window/packages/core) | Zod schemas, seed data, replay (`buildStateAtTime`), alerts, JSON + `localStorage` helpers |
| [`apps/web`](file:///C:/Users/Sunwoo/.cursor/projects/empty-window/apps/web) | Vite + React 19 + TS: dark command UI, **Google Maps** + **MarkerClusterer**, timeline scrub/playback, boards + CSV export, admin CRUD + **map pick** |
| [`apps/api`](file:///C:/Users/Sunwoo/.cursor/projects/empty-window/apps/api) | Fastify + Prisma + **PostgreSQL/PostGIS** (Docker), JWT login, `GET/PUT /api/ops`, `GET /api/audit`, audit rows on save |

**Note:** The API stores the operational picture as validated JSON in Prisma (not geometry columns yet). PostGIS is available via the Docker image for when you add real spatial queries/migrations.

### Run locally

1. **Install** (from repo root):
   ```bash
   npm install
   ```

2. **Maps (optional but recommended):** copy [`apps/web/.env.example`](file:///C:/Users/Sunwoo/.cursor/projects/empty-window/apps/web/.env.example) → `apps/web/.env` and set `VITE_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY` (and optionally `VITE_GOOGLE_MAP_ID`).

3. **Web:**
   ```bash
   npm run dev
   ```
   Opens Vite (e.g. http://127.0.0.1:5173). If `VITE_API_URL` is unset, **`/api` is proxied to port 3001**.

4. **DB + API (optional):**
   ```bash
   docker compose up -d
   copy apps\api\.env.example apps\api\.env
   ```
   Edit `apps/api/.env` if needed, then:
   ```bash
   cd apps/api && npx prisma generate && npx prisma db push && npm run dev
   ```
   Default admin: `admin@local.test` / `changeme` (overridable via `ADMIN_EMAIL` / `ADMIN_PASSWORD`).

5. In the UI: **API login** → loads remote snapshot; **Push to API** writes the current dataset and appends an **audit** entry.

### Behaviour highlights

- **Layers:** people / equipment / sites toggles in the header.
- **Timeline:** scrub or play; uses `homeCoordinates` / `homeLocationLabel` + event `meta.patch` for movement replay.
- **Persistence:** browser **`localStorage`** by default; JSON import/export under **Admin → data**.
- **Map pick:** edit a person or equipment → **Pick on map** (stashes draft to the store, then click the map).

Shell output in this environment was empty, so run `npm install` and `npm run dev` on your machine to confirm everything compiles. If anything fails (e.g. Fastify/JWT types), paste the error and we can tighten typings or scripts.

input.description: Run npm install at monorepo root to capture errors/output

content.text:
Here’s what matters:

### Run `npm install` in the **monorepo root**

That’s the folder whose **`package.json` includes `"workspaces": ["apps/*", "packages/*"]`**.

For the project we set up, that path is:

**`C:\Users\Sunwoo\.cursor\projects\empty-window`**

In PowerShell:

```powershell
cd C:\Users\Sunwoo\.cursor\projects\empty-window
npm install
```

Do **not** rely on only:

- `apps\web` — you *can* run `npm install` there, but it won’t install/link the whole workspace the way the root does.
- Some other folder that only has a single app and no root `package.json` — installs will fail or look “wrong.”

### Quick check you’re in the right place

That directory should contain **all** of these:

- `package.json` (root)
- `apps\web\package.json`
- `apps\api\package.json`
- `packages\core\package.json`

If you don’t see those, you’re not in the repo root (or the project lives somewhere else).

### If it still “doesn’t work”

Copy the **full terminal output** (and the path shown by `cd` / `Get-Location`). Typical issues:

- **Not in the root** (see above).
- **No / old Node** — use current LTS (e.g. **Node 20+**): `node -v`
- **npm workspaces** — needs a reasonably recent **npm** (7+): `npm -v`

Once `npm install` succeeds at the root, start the UI with:

```powershell
npm run dev
```

(still from the same root folder).
